---
title: "Numerical Analysis"
subtitle: "Foundations and Exercises"
author: 
  - name: "TANAKA, Kazuaki (田中 一成)"
    email: "k.tanaka@aoni.waseda.jp"
    affiliations:
      - name: "Global Center for Science and Engineering, Waseda University"
format: 
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, ../030/custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    html-math-method: mathjax
    chalkboard:
      storage: chalkboardData_4th
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-image: "../030/images/image1.png"
      data-background-size: "contain"
      data-background-opacity: "0.15"
---

## Before We Start {.smaller}

```{=html}
<div class="mission-tip" style="margin-top:0.6em; margin-bottom:0.8em;">
  <div class="mission-tip-icon">📥</div>
  <div class="mission-tip-content">
    <strong>Please clone (or pull) the course materials repository right now.</strong>
  </div>
</div>

<div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.5em; margin-bottom:0.8em;">
  <div>
    <div style="font-size:0.78em; color:#888; margin-bottom:0.2em;">First time</div>
    <pre style="margin:0; background:#1e1e2e; color:#cdd6f4; border-radius:8px; padding:0.5em 1em; font-size:0.78em; line-height:1.5;"><code>git clone https://github.com/waseda-num-analysis-2026/materials</code></pre>
  </div>
  <div>
    <div style="font-size:0.78em; color:#888; margin-bottom:0.2em;">Already cloned</div>
    <pre style="margin:0; background:#1e1e2e; color:#cdd6f4; border-radius:8px; padding:0.5em 1em; font-size:0.78em; line-height:1.5;"><code>cd materials
git pull</code></pre>
  </div>
</div>
<p style="font-size:0.78em; color:#666; margin:0.3em 0 0.6em 0.2em;">💡 Not sure how? Just ask your AI — it can run these commands for you.</p>

<div style="background:#f0f4ff; border-left:3px solid #4472C4; border-radius:6px; padding:0.6em 1em; font-size:0.82em;">
  <strong>Today's materials</strong> are inside <code>040/</code>:
  <table style="margin-top:0.4em; border-collapse:collapse; width:100%;">
    <tr><td style="padding:0.15em 0.6em 0.15em 0;"><code>4th-handout.qmd</code></td><td style="color:#555;">📘 <strong>main material</strong> — full content of this lecture</td></tr>
    <tr><td style="padding:0.15em 0.6em 0.15em 0;"><code>4th-handout.html</code></td><td style="color:#555;">rendered handout — viewable in browser</td></tr>
    <tr><td style="padding:0.15em 0.6em 0.15em 0;"><code>4th.qmd</code></td><td style="color:#555;">slides (this file) — exercises &amp; instructions only</td></tr>
    <tr><td style="padding:0.15em 0.6em 0.15em 0;"><code>4th.html</code></td><td style="color:#555;">rendered slides</td></tr>
  </table>
</div>
```

## Today's Main Material {.smaller}

```{=html}
<div style="background:#e8f5e9; border-left:4px solid #2e7d32; border-radius:8px; padding:0.8em 1em; margin-bottom:0.7em;">
  <div style="font-size:0.82em; color:#1b5e20; font-weight:700; letter-spacing:0.04em;">LECTURE 4</div>
  <div style="font-size:1.05em; margin-top:0.2em;"><strong>Vector Norms and Matrix Norms</strong></div>
  <div style="font-size:0.84em; color:#455a64; margin-top:0.25em;">
    The handout is the textbook. These slides are only for class logistics and assignments.
  </div>
</div>

<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr; gap:0.8em;">
  <div style="background:#fff8e1; border:1px solid #ffe082; border-radius:10px; padding:0.75em 0.9em;">
    <div style="font-size:0.8em; color:#e65100; font-weight:700;">Today you will submit</div>
    <ul style="font-size:0.83em; line-height:1.55; margin:0.4em 0 0 1.2em;">
      <li>Ex 4.0: AI Q&amp;A blocks in the handout</li>
      <li>Ex 4.1: proof of norm properties</li>
      <li>Ex 4.2: numerical verification</li>
      <li>Ex 4.3: random-search estimate of matrix norms</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Reminder — Session 3 Assignments {.smaller}

```{=html}
<div style="background:#fff3e0; border-left:4px solid #ef6c00; border-radius:8px; padding:0.75em 1em; margin-bottom:0.7em;">
  <div style="font-size:0.84em; color:#bf360c; font-weight:700; letter-spacing:0.04em;">REMINDER</div>
  <div style="font-size:0.95em; margin-top:0.2em;">
    Ex 3.0–3.3 deadlines were extended to <strong>May 14 (Thu), 23:59 JST</strong>.
  </div>
</div>

<div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.55em; font-size:0.86em;">
  <div style="background:#f3e5f5; border-left:4px solid #7b1fa2; border-radius:6px; padding:0.55em 0.85em;">
    🎥 Ex 3.2 video is still required. Pair work using the video will be held in <strong>Session 5 or later</strong>.
  </div>
  <div style="background:#e3f2fd; border-left:4px solid #1976d2; border-radius:6px; padding:0.55em 0.85em;">
    If you have grading or assignment questions, reply in the relevant GitHub Issue or ask in Mattermost <code>#question</code>.
  </div>
</div>
```

## Exercise 4.0 — Evolve the Handout with AI {.smaller .exercise-slide}

<div style="padding-left: 2em; margin-top: 0.5em; margin-bottom: 0.4em;">
  <strong>Repository:</strong> same as Ex 4.1
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Submit:</strong> <code>4th-handout.qmd</code> (your edited copy)
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong style="color: #d84315;">Deadline: May 14 (Thu), 23:59</strong>
</div>

While studying `4th-handout.qmd`, **insert at least 3 Q&A blocks** of your
own — questions you actually had while reading, answered with the help of
GitHub Copilot (or another AI) following the protocol in
[`AI_TUTOR.md`](https://github.com/waseda-num-analysis-2026/materials/blob/main/AI_TUTOR.md).

**How to submit**

1. Copy `materials/040/4th-handout.qmd` into the root of your **Ex 4** repository
2. Open it in VS Code, then for each question:
   - First prime the chat once with `AI_TUTOR.md`
   - Highlight the line you don't understand
   - Press `⌘L` / `Ctrl+L`, then ask the AI to add a Q&A block
   - Verify the AI's answer, then commit the inserted Q&A block
3. Repeat for **≥ 3 distinct questions**, then `git push`

<div style="margin-top:0.5em; padding:0.5em 0.7em; background:#fff8e1; border-left:4px solid #f9a825; border-radius:6px; font-size:0.85em;">
  💡 The Q&A block format is explained at the top of <code>4th-handout.qmd</code>.
</div>

## Exercise 4.1 — Prove the Standard Norms Are Norms {.smaller .exercise-slide}

<div style="padding-left: 2em; margin-top: 0.5em; margin-bottom: 0.4em;">
  <strong>Repository:</strong> Ex 4 — <a href="https://classroom.github.com/a/IIB7mFi7">GitHub Classroom link</a>
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Edit:</strong> <code>ex4-1.qmd</code>
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong style="color: #d84315;">Deadline: May 14 (Thu), 23:59</strong>
</div>

Prove that $\ell^1$, $\ell^2$, and $\ell^\infty$ are vector norms on
$\mathbb{R}^n$.

For each norm, verify:

1. Positivity
2. Homogeneity
3. Triangle inequality

<div style="margin-top:0.5em; padding:0.55em 0.85em; background:#ffebee; border-left:4px solid #c62828; border-radius:6px; font-size:0.85em;">
  ⚠️ <strong>AI is not recommended for the proof itself.</strong>
  You may use AI to convert handwritten equations to LaTeX, but the mathematical reasoning should be yours.
</div>

## Exercise 4.2 — Verify Norm Inequalities Numerically {.smaller .exercise-slide}

<div style="padding-left: 2em; margin-top: 0.5em; margin-bottom: 0.4em;">
  <strong>Repository:</strong> same as Ex 4.1
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Edit:</strong> <code>ex4-2.qmd</code>
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong style="color: #d84315;">Deadline: May 14 (Thu), 23:59</strong>
</div>

Confirm the following inequalities numerically using random vectors:

$$
\|x\|_2 \leq \|x\|_1 \leq \sqrt{n}\,\|x\|_2,
\qquad
\|x\|_\infty \leq \|x\|_1 \leq n\,\|x\|_\infty,
$$

$$
\|x\|_\infty \leq \|x\|_2 \leq \sqrt{n}\,\|x\|_\infty.
$$

**Minimum requirement**

- Test several dimensions, e.g. `n = 2, 5, 10, 50`
- Use many random vectors for each dimension
- Report clearly whether all tests passed

## Exercise 4.3 — Random Search for Matrix Norms {.smaller .exercise-slide}

<div style="padding-left: 2em; margin-top: 0.5em; margin-bottom: 0.4em;">
  <strong>Repository:</strong> same as Ex 4.1
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Edit:</strong> <code>ex4-3.qmd</code>
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong style="color: #d84315;">Deadline: May 14 (Thu), 23:59</strong>
</div>

Estimate the induced matrix norm $\|A\|_p$ from below by sampling random vectors.

<div style="font-size:0.78em; color:#666; margin:0.15em 0 0.45em 0;">
  AI use is allowed, but you must verify and explain the results yourself.
</div>

- Choose at least **two** matrices $A$; include one $2 \times 2$ example for
  geometric discussion.
- For $p = 1, 2, \infty$, sample many nonzero vectors and compute
  $r_p(x)=\|Ax\|_p/\|x\|_p$.
- For one $2 \times 2$ matrix, try several sample sizes, e.g.
  `N = 10, 100, 1000, 10000`; report the sampled maximum, true value, and
  relative gap.
- Compare the sampled maximum with the true value: is it above, below, or
  equal? Explain your answer. Then discuss why one case is close and another
  case is not.

## Exercise 4.3 — Optional Challenge {.smaller .exercise-slide}

For $p = 2$, do the following extra analysis for one $2 \times 2$ matrix:

$$
\|A\|_2 = \sqrt{\lambda_{\max}(A^T A)}.
$$

- Compute $\lambda_{\max}(A^T A)$ and confirm that it gives the same value
  as `numpy.linalg.norm(A, ord=2)`.
- Compute an eigenvector $v_{\max}$ of $A^T A$ for $\lambda_{\max}$, and
  check the stretch ratio $\|Av_{\max}\|_2 / \|v_{\max}\|_2$.
- Compare $v_{\max}$ with the best direction found by your random search.
- Briefly explain why this direction gives the maximum stretching factor.

::: {.mission-box}
This bonus is beyond today's main lecture. It is for students who want to
connect matrix norms with eigenvalues and maximum stretching directions.
:::

## Assignments and Preparation for Next Lecture {.smaller}

```{=html}
<div class="assign-list">
  <div class="assign-card">
    <div class="assign-num">1</div>
    <div class="assign-body">
      <p>Accept the <strong>Ex 4 GitHub Classroom</strong> link: <a href="https://classroom.github.com/a/IIB7mFi7">https://classroom.github.com/a/IIB7mFi7</a></p>
      <ul>
        <li>One repository will contain Ex 4.0, 4.1, 4.2, and 4.3</li>
        <li>Edit <code>ex4-1.qmd</code>, <code>ex4-2.qmd</code>, <code>ex4-3.qmd</code> + your copy of <code>4th-handout.qmd</code> for Ex 4.0</li>
        <li><strong>Deadline:</strong> <span class="assign-due">May 14 (Thu), 23:59</span></li>
        <li>Submit by <code>git push</code> — the last commit before the deadline is graded</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="assign-card">
    <div class="assign-num">2</div>
    <div class="assign-body">
      <p>Keep working on <strong>Ex 3.0–3.3</strong> if not finished yet</p>
      <ul>
        <li>Extended deadline: <strong>May 14 (Thu), 23:59 JST</strong></li>
        <li>Ex 3.2 video pair work will be announced later</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```
